item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not understand why transcribing the contents of the books is necessary for its containment. I might be missing something important, but it feels like this book could just be locked in a safe and not much else needs to be done. Maybe the foundation could find the occasional topic to be important, but I don't understand why it would need an in depth log needs to be kept of of these if they seem to be random.
I also don't fully understand why one particular doctor has to be in charge of this. We already saw that a doctor can die as a result of its contents, why list a name in the containment procedures if any doctor can be replaced? I think it would make more sense to list the transcriber as a more generic figure than one specific doctor who's name we are not even aloud to know.
Finally, I don't full understand how the final incident changed this object's class. Danger is not (or at least should not) be a factor in containment procedures. From what I can understand, this pretty much follows the box test to a key and just be locked away, if it wasn't for the need to record everything for some unknown reason.
Forgive me, I know I'm complaining at a six year old article and the author has probably written better since, but there are just a few details that I just think don't make sense. Overall, it is an interesting idea, but I feel like there is something I'm not getting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You lost me with the last addendum. The second to last addendum was weak as well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would an extended test log kill this?
I'm not really the first person to think "Hey, people could add to this.", since it seems Zetaprime9699 has some ideas at least.
I guess that'd be down to the author though. I'm just curious to see if people would think it's a bit much.

-Edit- Slightly related Pokémon theory:

Slowpoke is called the Dopey Pokemon, and every Pokedex entry mentions it being absent minded and airy. Imagine this though: they are so slow to react to the world because their brains are sensing everything that is happening in the universe that their mortal minds cannot possibly begin to be able to process this constant bombardment of information, explaining their lack of attachment to the world around them. When a Slowpoke is bitten on the head by a Shellder and becomes a Slowking, Shellder's venom actually impairs Slowpoke's brain and brings it to a level it can function, turning it into one of the most intelligent Pokemon in the world.

item.title: How To Kill SCP-682
item.content:encoded: It had to be said. I am very sorry but someone had to do it… Anyway, this is an excellent article (the How To Ascend bit aside, my brain immediately begun giving me suggestions for what kind of topics subsequent incarnations of the book could discuss) and I felt no hesitation upvoting it. Nice job! One comment: I think it would feel right for the article to mention the Foundation trying to determine whether there are other copies of this SCP in the wild. Difficult to comprehend for most or not, I would be rather worried not knowing whether someone out there happens to be reading How To Do Something Absolutely Horrifying With Minimal Effort this very moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, +100 is cool and surprising. Thanks for the comments. Maybe I'll even do a second SCP after this… year and a month hiatus.

As for edits, just run a PM by me and I will most likely be cool with it.

I like it, even though I could swear it reminds me of…something. Gah, maybe it is that anomalous item that was mentioned. Time to reread that article. +1.

My plus one puts this at one hundred, so congrats!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's not tagged as a collab, so I believe you'd need to PM the author to check your ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I free to edit this, or at least the list? I have some nice ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooh, I thoroughly enjoyed this one, and the ending was perfect. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How to read the How to book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do believe this would be related:
http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=007VM8NZxkI

And on that note, where it has nearly ultra-descriptive descriptions of muscle movements, I'd imagine that those would only be detailed in the books as 'fluff', such as something simple as "How to drink from a cup", and will be more generalized in a broader-scale book such as "How to tend to a farm"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the best part is that, while we don't know what "How to Ascend" was, the Foundation doesn't either.

The fact that it's probably driving them crazy amuses me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started out interesting, not quite upvote-worthy. Then the mentions of 'How To Ascend' happened, and that was interesting too, and then my god what is in that book why, how, so many questions. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, accidental imitation does seem like a very sincere form of flattery! Thank you for the kind words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I apologize. I know when to use apostrophes usually, but I was trying to get the revision out quickly before I had to leave, and for some reason, I threw one in there without thinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this idea so much I accidentally stole it. Top notch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent for a first post. The only error I see in the writing itself is "amount" should be "number."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I felt a disturbance in the Force…

Usually, I'm not a grammar nazi, and just let it slide (unless it's so terribad everyone else has already pounced on it, so I may as well get on that bandwagon), but pluralizing with an apostrophe has just always been the one thing that makes me unconditionally rage. Not cliches, not Expungement in the Containment Procedures, not even "Can we use it on 682?", just sticking apostrophes where they were never meant to go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Test's"?

Okay, if you're trying to pluralize a word and you stick an apostrophe in it, guess what: YOU'RE FUCKING DOING IT WRONG

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ladders can be pretty depressing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a… "cult" is the best word I can use, of people that actually do/did this to increase oxygen to the brain as they think it increases intelligence.

They often die of brain viruses…

I think "How to Acend" was a book on ladders, and the Dr. did it out of depression.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh Jesus, the how to ascend bit-

The lead-up was very suspenseful, and the actual act-agh, just left me with a very, very uncomfortable feeling.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking that exact same thing. This really does feel more like an expansion of that Anomalous Item than a brand new thing. Not that this is a bad thing, I think this is a good expansion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent. My one question is, is the new Head of Research now the Dr. Blackboxes listed in the containment procedures? I would assume the ascended Dr. Former Blackboxes is no longer capable of fulfilling his duties. It was a good way to keep one person interacting with the SCP, I'll say that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After its daily transformation, Dr. ██████ is to remove the object from its display case and transcribe the entirety of SCP-1161's contents.

Is only Dr. ██████ allowed to do the transcriptions because s/he's been vetted as trustworthy enough to be allowed to read potentially dangerous information? Because if not, I'd think that the job of transcription would be rotated among junior personnel, since the job is boring and tedious.

The various descriptions printed inside the book are incredibly detailed, to the point of describing which specific muscles to contract when performing a physical movement.

I'd think that this level of detail would bloat up the size of the book a whole lot.

The note read "It will be next. Do you value this world?", and was visible to all who viewed the page.

If I was an O5 I'd be paranoid enough to worry that that the message wasn't referring to "How to Ascend", but was referring to a disaster that could only be avoided by building the wooden rocking chair described in the book. Heheheh.

On ██/██/████ Dr. ██████ was found lying in his quarters with several puncture wounds in his scalp, which upon further inspection, were found to extend into his brain. A battery powered drill gripped in his hand suggests that the injuries were self inflicted.

Dr. Peter Venkman: Egon, this reminds me of the time you tried to drill a hole through your head. Remember that?

Dr. Egon Spengler: That would have worked if you hadn't stopped me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, VERY well done, especially for a first SCP. I am beyond impressed, and I hope you continue your writing on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who would've guessed such encyclopedic knowledge of Nethack could be so dangerous? This gets a (redundant) plus sign from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really nice job with the revision. Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot. One change, though—the sentence "If a person touches this book after transformation…" is incredibly awkward; I think you could do with out the if and just say "SCP-1161 is only legible to the first individual to touch the object after its daily transformation" or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the the anomalous items is kind of similar, but this just takes the idea to a new level. Love it, +++1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job, I have upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fairly drastically edited the final portion. Hope it's an improvement! Also please notify me of any grammatical errors or wonky scientific terms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay so you were going for enlightenment. Hm, you could perhaps make that more… weird or scary? The first addendum mentioned 'How to ascend', which sounded pretty ominous to me; since this is an SCP, I thought it wouldn't exactly mean enlightenment in the traditional sense but something more weird / horrible.

Well anyway, for a first SCP I think this is pretty solid. I haven't even posted my first one yet, but I hope it's going to be like this one quality-wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As for what happened in the final redaction, I wrote it under the assumption that either he or the book emitted a flash of light and the Doctor ascended to some sort of higher state of consciousness. But in hindsight, that does seem like a tad bit much. I am working to fix it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I like that you got the morbid topics out of the way first, and then followed up with "How to Ascend" which is really cool. Solid work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Yeah the last addendum is perhaps a bit much. May I ask why you added it? Do you know what happened exactly? Or is it written to add just a bit of creep?
Aside from that, it has a lot of potential. I mean I think it's pretty good right now, but I like how you could add another 'How-to' once in a while that can range from really creepy to really funny. I'm going to upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the suggestions. I will work on making it more interesting. Shouldn't take too long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed all instances of the incorrect usage of its/it's. Guess I skipped out on the day they were explaining the possessive nature of the word 'it'.

As for Addendum C, I will think on possibly revising it. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. I wasn't on board at first but I started seeing the potential towards the end when you started foreshadowing the "How To Ascend" but we just got a data redacted…. I'm thinking it just needs something more.

Not sure where you can take that now. Maybe instead of the guy apparently vanishing in a flash of light he reads the book cover to cover and leaves the room for awhile. When people ask him about what he read he doesn't explain it very well, or gives them a false story to throw them off. Eventually he gets caught preforming some horrible sacrifice ritual or hollows out his own head while trying to turn himself into a post-human computer or something… or maybe something more subtle where the Foundation locks him up because its clear he is under the influence of something and maaaaybe that influence is spreading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, and the execution isn't bad, either. There are a few grammar errors (especially it's/its), and I would suggest revising the third addendum - too much redaction and a little unexpected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP. Hopefully it is enjoyable.
